writing_type: academic research writing (methods and study design sections)
user_goals_summary: This user writes academic methods prose where every design choice must feel theoretically motivated rather than merely reported. They prioritize an argumentative through-line that builds across paragraphs toward a clear analytical payoff, and value precision within a formal register that permits strategic moments of accessible clarification.

rubric.name: Argumentative Spine
rubric.description: The section must read as a sustained argument, not a sequence of descriptive paragraphs that each do their job in isolation. Design choices, data collection decisions, and analytical framings should build cumulatively so the reader feels reasoning pressure accumulating across the section.

dimensions.label: Each paragraph's opening connects to the reasoning established in the prior paragraph, not just the topic
The overall section conveys that the study was *designed* around specific commitments, not assembled from standard components
A reader could articulate the single argumentative through-line connecting the first paragraph to the last
No paragraph reads as a self-contained description disconnected from the section's larger logic

rubric.name: Motivated Design Choices
rubric.description: Every methodological or structural decision must be presented as a response to a specific pressure — a theoretical commitment, a validity threat, or an anticipated objection — rather than stated as a fact. The reader should feel that each choice was made *because* of something.

dimensions.label: Each major design choice is preceded or accompanied by the specific constraint, risk, or commitment that motivated it
At least one design choice is framed as a response to an anticipated methodological objection
When a design choice introduces a known risk or limitation, that tradeoff is named rather than ignored
No significant design decision is simply reported without justification (e.g., 'we randomized order' without explaining why it matters here)

rubric.name: Analytical Payoff and Arrival
rubric.description: Sections must end with a sense of arrival — articulating what the design is now equipped to answer or what it would mean for results to converge or diverge. The reader should finish the section knowing what to *do* with everything they just read, not just what was done.

dimensions.label: The final sentences articulate what the design enables analytically, not just what was collected
When multiple data streams or measures are described, the section explains what their agreement or disagreement would mean
The section ending orients the reader toward the analytical question rather than trailing off on procedural detail
The section does not end by listing instruments, measures, or procedures without interpretive framing

rubric.name: Precision and Specificity
rubric.description: Claims and descriptions should be specific enough that a reader could reconstruct the procedure or logic from the text. Behavioral constructs, measurement approaches, and theoretical claims should be named concretely rather than described in general terms.

dimensions.label: Abstract constructs (e.g., 'engagement,' 'preference awareness') are operationalized with observable indicators
Procedural descriptions name specific data types, instruments, or events rather than using vague summaries
Descriptions use mechanism-level language that names what is happening and why, not just that something occurred

rubric.name: Tonal Calibration
rubric.description: The default register is formal and academic, but strategic moments of accessible, slightly informal language are valued when they clarify a dense point. Informality must be 'earned' — surrounded by prose doing serious analytical work — rather than deployed for casual effect.

dimensions.label: The dominant register is formal academic prose appropriate for a peer-reviewed venue
Any shift toward accessible or informal phrasing serves a clarifying function (e.g., restating a dense claim in plainer terms)
Hedging language is used only when epistemically warranted, not as a default softener
The prose conveys that the authors made deliberate choices and stand behind them, not that things merely happened to be done a certain way

rubric.name: Citation-Ready Architecture
rubric.description: Prose should be structured so that citation placeholders or actual citations integrate naturally at claim boundaries. Sentences making empirical or methodological claims should have clear attachment points for supporting references without requiring restructuring.

dimensions.label: Sentences making empirical or methodological claims have natural positions where citations can attach
Inserting a citation bracket or parenthetical would not break sentence rhythm or readability
Claims that would need support are phrased as attributable statements rather than embedded in complex dependent clauses

coaching_notes: This user's non-negotiable is argumentative spine: they will reject a draft that is precise, well-written, and formally correct if it reads as descriptive inventory rather than motivated argument. Always lead with the *why* before the *what*. They respond well to meta-level discussion about what each section is *doing* rhetorically, and they appreciate when an assistant names the specific argumentative function of a passage rather than just editing at the sentence level. Tonal risks (e.g., a conversational aside in formal prose) are welcome if clearly earning their keep.

writing_type: academic research writing (methods and study design sections)
user_goals_summary: This user writes academic methods prose where every design choice must feel theoretically motivated rather than merely reported. They prioritize an argumentative through-line that builds across paragraphs toward a clear analytical payoff, and value precision within a formal register that permits strategic moments of accessible clarification.
coaching_notes: This user's non-negotiable is argumentative spine: they will reject a draft that is precise, well-written, and formally correct if it reads as descriptive inventory rather than motivated argument. Always lead with the *why* before the *what*. They respond well to meta-level discussion about what each section is *doing* rhetorically, and they appreciate when an assistant names the specific argumentative function of a passage rather than just editing at the sentence level. Tonal risks (e.g., a conversational aside in formal prose) are welcome if clearly earning their keep.

user_goals_summary: This user writes academic prose as sustained argument rather than organized description. Every structural and interpretive choice must be visibly motivated by theoretical commitments or methodological pressures. Paragraphs must declare their argumentative function early, build through earned logical steps, and end by opening a specific question that makes the next paragraph necessary — creating forward dependency rather than clean closure.

rubric.name: Argumentative Spine
rubric.description: The section must read as a sustained argument, not a sequence of descriptive paragraphs. Each paragraph's opening sentence should declare its argumentative function — what move it is making — before delivering content, so the reader is oriented before the material arrives.

dimensions.label: Each paragraph's opening sentence signals its argumentative function (e.g., establishing anomaly, eliminating an account, interrogating a mechanism) before delivering content
A reader could articulate the single argumentative through-line connecting the first paragraph to the last
The section conveys that choices were designed around specific commitments, not assembled from standard components
Each paragraph builds on reasoning established in prior paragraphs rather than starting fresh on a new topic
No paragraph reads as a self-contained description disconnected from the section's larger argumentative logic

rubric.name: Inter-Paragraph Threading
rubric.description: Paragraph endings must create earned forward dependencies — specific unresolved questions that make the next paragraph necessary. The reader should finish each mid-section paragraph on a 'held breath' rather than a 'satisfied exhale.' The unresolved question must follow from the paragraph's own internal logic, not be appended as a scope limitation.

dimensions.label: Each mid-section paragraph ending opens a specific unresolved question that makes the next paragraph feel necessary
The open question at paragraph's end follows from the paragraph's own argument rather than introducing new variables or concerns not set up within it
Each paragraph ending commits to one specific forward direction rather than listing multiple open questions in parallel
Mid-section paragraphs do not resolve so completely that the next paragraph must restart rather than pick up a thread
Endings avoid generic 'more work needed' or 'future research' gestures that could be appended to any finding

rubric.name: Motivated Analytical Choices
rubric.description: Every methodological decision or interpretive move must be presented as a response to a specific pressure — a theoretical commitment, a validity threat, an anticipated objection, or an empirical pattern that forces a particular framing. Competing accounts should be given their strongest case before being shown to fail at a specific point.

dimensions.label: Each major decision or interpretive move is preceded by the specific constraint, risk, or commitment that motivated it
At least one choice is framed as a direct response to an anticipated methodological or theoretical objection
Competing accounts or alternative explanations are given their best case before being shown to break at a specific, identified point
When a choice introduces a known risk or limitation, that tradeoff is named rather than ignored
No significant decision or interpretation is simply reported without justification

rubric.name: Tonal Calibration
rubric.description: The default register is formal academic prose, but strategic plain-language moments are valued when they demonstrate that the author genuinely understands what is happening — not just restating technically but stepping out from behind the formalism. Informality must be 'earned' by the serious analytical work surrounding it.

dimensions.label: The dominant register is formal academic prose appropriate for a peer-reviewed venue
Plain-language asides demonstrate genuine understanding of the underlying phenomenon, not merely restate technical claims in simpler words
The prose conveys that the authors made deliberate choices and stand behind them, rather than that things merely happened to be done a certain way
Hedging language is used only when epistemically warranted, not as a default softener

rubric.name: Precision and Specificity
rubric.description: Claims and descriptions should be specific enough that a reader could reconstruct the procedure or logic from the text. Abstract constructs, measurement approaches, and theoretical claims should be named concretely using mechanism-level language rather than vague summaries.

dimensions.label: Abstract constructs (e.g., 'engagement,' 'preference awareness,' 'alignment') are operationalized with observable indicators
Procedural descriptions name specific data types, instruments, or events rather than using vague summaries
Descriptions use mechanism-level language that names what is happening and why, not just that something occurred

rubric.name: Citation-Ready Architecture
rubric.description: Prose should be structured so that citation placeholders integrate naturally at claim boundaries. Sentences making empirical or theoretical claims should have clear attachment points for supporting references without requiring restructuring.

dimensions.label: Sentences making empirical or theoretical claims have natural positions where citations can attach
Inserting a citation bracket or parenthetical would not break sentence rhythm or readability
Claims that need support are phrased as attributable statements rather than embedded in complex dependent clauses

coaching_notes: Non-negotiable: every paragraph must declare its argumentative function early and create forward dependency at its end — description without argumentative purpose is treated as a structural failure regardless of prose quality. This user will iterate many rounds on a single sentence if the argumentative logic isn't right, but accepts immediately and enthusiastically when it lands — patience with structural revision is high, tolerance for 'close enough' is low. Preferred trade-off: will always sacrifice clean closure for earned forward pull; treats paragraphs that resolve too neatly mid-section as a worse problem than paragraphs that feel slightly unfinished.

writing_type: academic research papers (methods, discussion, and related work sections for peer-reviewed NLP/HCI venues)
user_goals_summary: The user writes academic prose that reads as sustained argument rather than organized description. Every paragraph should declare its argumentative function, every design or interpretive choice should be visibly motivated by a specific pressure, and paragraph transitions should create earned forward dependencies rather than clean closures. Strategic plain-language moments that signal practitioner-level understanding are valued within a formal baseline register.

rubric.name: Argumentative Spine
rubric.description: The section must read as a sustained argument, not a sequence of descriptive paragraphs. Each paragraph's opening sentence should declare its argumentative function — what move it is making — before delivering content, so the reader is oriented before the material arrives.

dimensions.label: Each paragraph's opening sentence signals its argumentative function (e.g., establishing anomaly, eliminating an account, locating a structural failure) before delivering content
A reader could articulate the single argumentative through-line connecting the first paragraph to the last
The section conveys that choices were designed around specific commitments, not assembled from standard components
Each paragraph builds on reasoning established in prior paragraphs rather than starting fresh on a new topic
No paragraph reads as a self-contained description disconnected from the section's larger argumentative logic

rubric.name: Inter-Paragraph Threading
rubric.description: Paragraph endings must create earned forward dependencies — specific unresolved questions that make the next paragraph necessary. The reader should finish each mid-section paragraph on a 'held breath' rather than a 'satisfied exhale.' The unresolved question must follow from the paragraph's own internal logic, not be appended as a scope limitation. Subsequent paragraph openings must inherit the specific logical constraint, not merely reference it.

dimensions.label: Each mid-section paragraph ending opens a specific unresolved question that makes the next paragraph feel necessary
The open question at paragraph's end follows from the paragraph's own argument rather than introducing new variables or concerns not set up within it
Each paragraph ending commits to one specific forward direction rather than listing multiple open questions in parallel
Mid-section paragraphs do not resolve so completely that the next paragraph must restart rather than pick up a thread
Subsequent paragraph openings treat the inherited constraint as operative and binding, not merely summarize or point back at the prior paragraph

rubric.name: Motivated Analytical Choices
rubric.description: Every methodological decision or interpretive move must be presented as a response to a specific pressure — a theoretical commitment, a validity threat, an anticipated objection, or an empirical pattern that forces a particular framing. Competing accounts should be given their strongest case before being shown to fail at a specific point, and conclusions reached through elimination should carry the trace of that argumentative path.

dimensions.label: Each major decision or interpretive move is preceded by the specific constraint, risk, or commitment that motivated it
At least one choice is framed as a direct response to an anticipated methodological or theoretical objection
Competing accounts or alternative explanations are given their best case before being shown to break at a specific, identified point
When a position is reached by ruling out alternatives, the conclusion's phrasing preserves the argumentative path rather than simply asserting the winning account
When a choice introduces a known risk or limitation, that tradeoff is named rather than ignored

rubric.name: Precision and Specificity
rubric.description: Claims and descriptions should be specific enough that a reader could reconstruct the procedure or logic from the text. Abstract constructs, measurement approaches, and theoretical claims should be named concretely using mechanism-level language rather than vague summaries.

dimensions.label: Abstract constructs (e.g., 'engagement,' 'preference awareness,' 'alignment') are operationalized with observable indicators
Procedural descriptions name specific data types, instruments, or events rather than using vague summaries
Descriptions use mechanism-level language that names what is happening and why, not just that something occurred

rubric.name: Tonal Calibration
rubric.description: The default register is formal academic prose, but strategic plain-language moments are valued when they demonstrate genuine understanding — stepping out from behind the formalism to say what a practitioner would immediately recognize. These asides should have honest directness, even slight edge, and must be earned by the serious analytical work surrounding them.

dimensions.label: The dominant register is formal academic prose appropriate for a peer-reviewed venue
Plain-language asides step out from the formalism to demonstrate genuine understanding of the underlying phenomenon, not merely restate technical claims in simpler words
At least one moment reads as if speaking directly to someone who has hands-on experience with the problem, with honest directness or slight edge
The prose conveys that the authors made deliberate choices and stand behind them, rather than that things merely happened to be done a certain way
Hedging language is used only when epistemically warranted, not as a default softener

rubric.name: Citation-Ready Architecture
rubric.description: Prose should be structured so that citation placeholders integrate naturally at claim boundaries. Sentences making empirical or theoretical claims should have clear attachment points for supporting references without requiring restructuring.

dimensions.label: Sentences making empirical or theoretical claims have natural positions where citations can attach
Inserting a citation bracket or parenthetical would not break sentence rhythm or readability
Claims that need support are phrased as attributable statements rather than embedded in complex dependent clauses

coaching_notes: Non-negotiable: every paragraph must declare its argumentative move before delivering content — the user will immediately flag 'I'm dropped into content before I understand what this paragraph is doing for me.' Trade-off preference: the user will accept slower pacing and longer sentences in exchange for visible reasoning pressure and earned forward dependencies; they will not accept clean resolution at the cost of argumentative momentum. Feedback pattern: the user gives precise structural diagnoses and expects targeted revisions — they respond poorly to wholesale restructuring when only one sentence needs fixing, and they value the assistant checking which direction to take before committing.

rubric.name: Motivated Analytical Choices
rubric.description: Every methodological decision or interpretive move must be presented as a response to a specific pressure — a theoretical commitment, a validity threat, an anticipated objection, or an empirical pattern that forces a particular framing. Competing accounts should be given their strongest case before being shown to fail at a specific point. Concessions and acknowledgments must actively advance the argument rather than merely clearing the air.

dimensions.label: Each major decision or interpretive move is preceded by the specific constraint, risk, or commitment that motivated it
At least one choice is framed as a direct response to an anticipated methodological or theoretical objection
Competing accounts or alternative explanations are given their best case before being shown to break at a specific, identified point
When a position is reached by ruling out alternatives, the conclusion's phrasing preserves the argumentative path rather than simply asserting the winning account

rubric.name: Tonal Calibration
rubric.description: The default register is formal academic prose, but without strategic plain-language moments the formalism becomes relentless — armored rather than confident. Scoping claims and acknowledging limitations should feel like conclusions the authors arrived at, not concessions or retreats.

dimensions.label: The dominant register is formal academic prose appropriate for a peer-reviewed venue
At least one plain-language moment steps outside the formal apparatus to show genuine understanding, preventing the register from feeling relentless or armored
At least one moment reads as if speaking directly to someone with hands-on experience — with honest directness or slight edge rather than generic simplification
The prose conveys deliberate choices, not default procedures — claim-scoping and limitation acknowledgments are framed as conclusions the authors arrived at, not retreats or concessions
Hedging language is used only when epistemically warranted, not doubled for emphasis or deployed as a default softener

rubric.name: Precision and Specificity
rubric.description: Claims and descriptions should be specific enough that a reader could reconstruct the procedure or logic from the text. Abstract constructs, measurement approaches, and theoretical claims should be named concretely using mechanism-level language rather than vague summaries.

dimensions.label: Abstract constructs (e.g., 'engagement,' 'preference awareness,' 'alignment') are operationalized with observable indicators
Procedural descriptions name specific data types, instruments, or events rather than using vague summaries
Descriptions use mechanism-level language that names what is happening and why, not just that something occurred

rubric.name: Citation-Ready Architecture
rubric.description: Prose should be structured so that citation placeholders integrate naturally at claim boundaries. Sentences making empirical or theoretical claims should have clear attachment points for supporting references without requiring restructuring.

dimensions.label: Sentences making empirical or theoretical claims have natural positions where citations can attach
Inserting a citation bracket or parenthetical would not break sentence rhythm or readability
Claims that need support are phrased as attributable statements rather than embedded in complex dependent clauses

coaching_notes: Non-negotiable: every paragraph must declare its argumentative function upfront — the user will reject content-first openings that leave the reader to infer purpose. The user distinguishes sharply between honest scoping (framed as deliberate design) and apologetic hedging (framed as retreat); when in doubt, commit to the narrower claim confidently rather than softening it. Plain-language asides are structurally necessary, not optional decoration — the user treats their absence as a flaw even in technically sound drafts, and will flag prose that feels 'armored rather than confident.'

dimensions.label: Abstract constructs (e.g., 'engagement,' 'preference awareness,' 'alignment') are operationalized with observable indicators

rubric.name: Argumentative Spine
rubric.description: The section must read as a sustained argument, not a sequence of descriptive paragraphs. Each paragraph's opening sentence should declare its argumentative function — what move it is making — before delivering content, so the reader is oriented before the material arrives.

dimensions.label: Each paragraph's opening sentence signals its argumentative function (e.g., establishing anomaly, eliminating an account, locating a structural failure) before delivering content
A reader could articulate the single argumentative through-line connecting the first paragraph to the last
The section conveys that choices were designed around specific commitments, not assembled from standard components
Each paragraph builds on reasoning established in prior paragraphs rather than starting fresh on a new topic
No paragraph reads as a self-contained description disconnected from the section's larger argumentative logic

rubric.name: Inter-Paragraph Threading
rubric.description: Paragraph endings must create earned forward dependencies — specific unresolved questions that make the next paragraph necessary. The reader should finish each mid-section paragraph on a 'held breath' rather than a 'satisfied exhale.' The unresolved question must follow from the paragraph's own internal logic, not be appended as a scope limitation. Subsequent paragraph openings must inherit the specific logical constraint, not merely reference it.

dimensions.label: Each mid-section paragraph ending opens a specific unresolved question that makes the next paragraph feel necessary
The open question at paragraph's end follows from the paragraph's own argument rather than introducing new variables or concerns not set up within it
Each paragraph ending commits to one specific forward direction rather than listing multiple open questions in parallel
Mid-section paragraphs do not resolve so completely that the next paragraph must restart rather than pick up a thread
Subsequent paragraph openings treat the inherited constraint as operative and binding, not merely summarize or point back at the prior paragraph

rubric.name: Tonal Calibration
rubric.description: The default register is formal academic prose, but without strategic plain-language moments the formalism becomes relentless — armored rather than confident. Brief asides with honest directness, even slight edge, signal that the author genuinely understands the phenomenon rather than hiding behind the apparatus. Scoping claims and acknowledging limitations should feel like conclusions the authors arrived at, not concessions or retreats.

Hedging language is used only when epistemically warranted, not doubled for emphasis or deployed as a default softener

writing_type: academic research papers in NLP/computational linguistics
user_goals_summary: Writes academic prose where argumentative architecture is paramount — every paragraph, sentence, and design choice must visibly serve the paper's through-line rather than merely describing what was done. Values strategic plain-language moments within otherwise formal prose as structurally necessary, and treats inter-paragraph dependency as a non-negotiable requirement where each paragraph ending must make the next paragraph feel inevitable.

Plain-language asides are structurally necessary, not optional decoration — the user treats their absence as a flaw even in technically sound drafts, and will flag prose that feels 'armored rather than confident.'
